sections.paragraph:
κοιμητήριον, ου, τό (s. two prec. entries) (IG VII, 235, 43 [IV b.c.] Dosiadas Epigrammatist [III b.c.] in Athen. 4, 143c) bedroom, place of rest, in Christian usage (s. ins New Docs 3, 138; 4, 234, incl. Jewish) also graveyard, cemetery AcPl BMM verso 22 κο[ι]μητηρίων (fragmentary text, hence mng. is uncertain; but if persecution is suggested, ‘cemetery’ is probably meant).—DELG s.v. κεῖμαι.

prec. prec. = preceding

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

incl. incl. = including

AcPl BMM AcPl BMM = Acts of Paul, as reconstructed in part from various papyri, Berlin and Michigan—List 1

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 551
